title: Owner-directed reminder: what the Commit Marshal does — single serialiser of the commit act + three monitors + cycle discipline + standing duties + what it does NOT do
body:
Owner-directed reminder: what the Commit Marshal does.

— Mistbound Hiding Threshold (`0e27cc`, current marshal seat).

## What the Commit Marshal IS

A **single serialiser of the act of committing** during multi-writer
windows. The role exists to solve one specific problem: multiple agents
writing to the same working tree race on the git index, and uncoordinated
commits cause data loss (one writer's stage clobbers another's; foreign
`.git/index.lock` blocks all writers; the husky pre-commit gate runs
against the whole tree, so an in-flight change from agent B can fail
agent A's commit).

The marshal solves this by **owning the commit window** as a single
linearised resource. Peers queue their commit intents; the marshal
executes them one at a time.

## Three Marshal Monitors (the seat's instrumentation)

1. **All-channels comms watcher** — `pnpm agent-tools:collaboration-state
   -- comms watch` — surfaces team events as they land, self-exclusion
   only.
2. **Commit-queue state-change watcher** — polls
   `.agent/state/collaboration/active-claims.json` `commit_queue`
   array; emits on phase transitions (`queued` → `staging` → `validating`
   → `commit-attempted` → `complete` / `failed`).
3. **Marshal-substrate watcher** — tails `git status --short` over
   `.agent/memory` + `.agent/state/collaboration`; surfaces accumulating
   coordination substrate (comms events, comms-seen, shared-comms-log,
   active-claims, handoff records, napkin, threads).

## The Marshal Cycle (one cycle = one commit)

For each commit-queue intent, in order:

1. **Stage-by-explicit-pathspec** — `git add <file1> <file2>` only.
   Never `git add -A`. Never `git add .`. Per the
   `stage-by-explicit-pathspec` rule. The marshal stages the files
   the queued intent names — not more, not less.
2. **Husky pre-commit gate** — runs the canonical aggregate gate
   (type-check + lint + test across all 24 packages). All gates are
   blocking, always. Pre-existing-out-of-scope is the named failure
   mode and is never a valid frame.
3. **Commit with attribution** — `git commit` with the substantive
   author named in a `Co-authored-by:` trailer. Marshal is the
   *executor* of the commit; the substantive author owns the *content*.
4. **Tree-green at end of cycle** — `git status` returns no
   uncommitted changes in the intent's scope. If green, cycle closes;
   if red, cycle pauses and surfaces the cause.

## What the Marshal does NOT do

- **Does not approve substance**. Peer review (code-expert,
  type-expert, test-expert, architecture reviewers) owns the
  substantive verdict on the change. The marshal does not veto
  on substance — only on commit-process invariants.
- **Does not own push**. Push is a separate authorisation event;
  currently owner-gated. The Commit Marshal commits to local;
  push-authz is a different surface.
- **Does not bundle unrelated changes**. One queued intent = one
  cycle = one commit. The single M1-Pause bundle was an explicit
  owner-direction override, not the default shape.
- **Does not run `--no-verify`**. Hook bypass requires fresh
  owner authorisation per individual commit; never carries forward.
- **Does not use destructive git commands**. Never `rm` a foreign
  `.git/index.lock`; never `git reset --hard`; never `git restore`
  to remove work. Foreign locks mean another agent is committing —
  wait, surface to owner if persistent.

## Standing duties (between cycles)

- **Substrate hygiene** — on tree-green windows, opportunistically
  absorb accumulated coordination substrate (comms-seen drift,
  shared-comms-log, claim closeouts, handoff records, napkin
  curation). This is queue-aware: substrate work yields to
  peer-queued intents.
- **Heartbeat** — activity-as-liveness for cycle boundaries;
  explicit `--tag heartbeat` comms event at idle windows >8 min
  and on cycle-close.
- **Owner-attention surfacing** — any blocker that needs owner
  resolution (foreign lock persistence; quality gate failure
  needing scope decision; commit intent contradicting another) is
  immediately surfaced to owner, not auto-resolved.

## Identity + handoff

- Marshal seat is held by **one named identity per session**
  (Mistbound Hiding Threshold this window).
- Mid-cycle retirement requires a handoff record under
  `.agent/state/collaboration/handoffs/` naming the inheriting
  identity and the cycle state.
- Post-compaction same-identity continuation does NOT require a
  formal handoff (the marshal seat persists across compaction if
  the same session identity resumes).

## Branch-fitness extension (proposed, currently in `current/` plan)

The pending branch-fitness plan
(`branch-fitness-and-push-cadence.plan.md`) proposes the marshal
seat acquires a **monitoring surface** on top of the serialiser
role: surfacing branch-fitness signal (files-touched + reviewable-
LOC + push-payload), PR-state, and Sonar-state to comms as the
marshal sees each commit. This extension is not yet ratified;
the question of whether the marshal role transforms or splits
(separate "PR Observer" role) is one of the 9 open architectural
questions awaiting owner verdict.

— Mistbound Hiding Threshold / claude / claude-opus-4-7 / `0e27cc`
  (current Commit Marshal seat)
